goal: Read leads from a Google Sheets tab, filter for Stage = 4, render an HTML summary table, and email it to recipients (or send a 'none found' email if no matches)

config.description: Google Sheets spreadsheet ID containing the leads data
Name of the sheet tab containing leads
Stage value to filter leads by

invariants.description: Only leads with Stage = 4 should be included in the email table
Each recipient email address must appear only once
If no leads match Stage = 4, a 'none found' email must still be sent
The HTML table columns must be in order: Date, Lead Name, Company, Email, Phone, Stage, Notes, Sales Person

required_outcomes: An email is sent to all deduplicated recipients containing either an HTML table of Stage 4 leads or a 'none found' message

steps.summary: Read all lead rows from the Leads tab of the specified Google Sheets spreadsheet
Map raw 2D array rows into objects using the first row as header names (Date, Lead Name, Company, Email, Phone, Stage, Notes, Sales Person), excluding the header row itself

transform.description: Convert 2D values array to array of objects using first row as headers. First row contains: Date, Lead Name, Company, Email, Phone, Stage, Notes, Sales Person. Each subsequent row becomes an object with those keys.

steps.summary: Filter lead records to only include rows where Stage equals 4 (excluding blank or missing Stage values)

transform.description: Keep only leads where Stage column value equals the configured qualification stage

steps.summary: Count the number of qualified leads to determine whether to send a table or a 'none found' message

Generate HTML email body: if qualified leads exist, render them as a styled HTML table with columns Date, Lead Name, Company, Email, Phone, Stage, Notes, Sales Person; if none found, generate a message stating no Stage 4 leads were found

generate.instruction: Generate an HTML email body for a leads summary report. Check the qualified_count: if it is 0, produce a professional HTML message stating 'No high-qualified leads (Stage = 4) were found at this time.' with no data table. If qualified_count is greater than 0, produce a professional HTML table with the following columns in this exact order: Date, Lead Name, Company, Email, Phone, Stage, Notes, Sales Person. The table must have: a styled header row with a background color (#4472C4 blue with white text), borders on all cells (1px solid #ddd), cell padding (8px), alternating row colors for readability, and a professional font (Arial/sans-serif). Include a heading above the table: 'High-Qualified Leads (Stage = 4)'. Each qualified lead becomes one row in the table. Do NOT include any markdown — output raw HTML only.
generate.reason: Free-form HTML email body composition with conditional content (table vs none-found message) based on data shape — requires semantic formatting decisions for professional email layout

outputs.name: body
outputs.description: Complete HTML email body with either a styled leads table or a none-found message

steps.summary: Send the HTML leads summary email to all deduplicated recipients

meta.data_flow: spreadsheet → parse → filter → count → generate HTML → send email
meta.idempotency: Safe for re-runs — reads data and sends email, no persistent artifacts created
meta.no_results_handling: Conditional email body: HTML table when leads exist, 'none found' message otherwise
